Install
gitomi/local-first-forge
Code Issues Pull Requests Projects Workflows
refs/gitomi/inbox/alice/laptop signed
Plan v1 release notes#b41a908 opened locally
open
Review sync admission rulespull request from feature/fsck
ready

Local-first collaboration

Gitomi

A Git-native forge for issues, pull requests, project boards, milestones, access control, and workflow runs. The source of truth is signed commits under refs/gitomi/*.

Get Started See the Model

Why Gitomi

Collaboration state that follows the repository.

Gitomi keeps social and automation state in normal Git refs, so a clone, fetch, push, bundle, mirror, or fork can carry the forge with the code.

Plain Git replication

Issues, PRs, projects, and runs move through ordinary Git transport. There is no central database to promote into the authority path.

Signed event history

Every user-facing change is a native signed commit. Reducers validate identity, sequence, RBAC, and causal context before projecting state.

Offline-friendly by design

Each device writes to its own inbox ref. Replicas merge deterministic projections when they reconnect and sync.

Storage model

Two planes, one repository.

Data plane

Branches, tags, trees, and blobs stay standard.

Gitomi does not put issue or workflow state into the working tree. Source code remains ordinary Git data.

Control plane

Signed append-only event commits live under refs/gitomi/inbox/*.

The current view is derived into a disposable local index that can be rebuilt from valid refs.

  1. WriteLocal actor signs an event commit.
  2. FetchRemote inbox refs land in staging.
  3. ValidateSignatures, event envelopes, chains, and RBAC are checked.
  4. ProjectAccepted events rebuild issues, PRs, boards, runs, and ACLs.

Product surface

Forge workflows without hosted lock-in.

Issues and pull requests

Open, label, assign, comment, review, and merge without moving collaboration state into a hosted database.

Project boards and milestones

Plan work through kanban boards, milestone status, and issue placement events that sync with the repository.

Local workflow runs

Discover native Gitomi workflows or GitHub Actions-compatible files and run them locally with signed results.

GitHub migration path

Import existing GitHub issues and pull requests, preserve legacy numbers, and export transitions back when needed.

Local web UI

Browse code, issues, pull requests, projects, workflows, events, refs, and repository diagnostics.

  • Code explorer
  • Review threads
  • Workflow runs

Quick start

Build the CLI and initialize a repository.

Build

Compile the Zig CLI from the repository checkout.

cd cli
zig build
./zig-out/bin/gt --help

Initialize

Attach Gitomi to an existing Git repository with a signed local device.

gt init --principal alice --device laptop
gt issue open --title "Write the release notes" --body "Collect highlights for v1."
gt issue list

Browse

Start the loopback web UI for code, issues, projects, events, workflows, and refs.

gt web

Reference

Designed for inspectability.

The normative design is split across product, ref layout, RBAC, workflows, and project web UI specs in the repository. The implemented CLI covers initialization, sync, fsck, events, issues, pull requests, comments, projects, milestones, actions, GitHub import/export, and the local web UI.

README CLI reference Product spec Ref spec
Gitomi stores forge state in Git, not around it.
